page: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. Memory capacity is 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory performance. The multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and the PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads reaching 4505.69 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 220.43 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25%. Large language model text generation is poor, falling in the bottom 10% for small and medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5n.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, balancing its high vCPU count with a substantial DDR4 memory footprint. The lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached EBS volumes, utilizing the 100 Gbps network baseline. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded software compilation, while it is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or real-time LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are weak. Qualitatively, the instance offers high memory density per vCPU, making it cost-efficient for memory-bound applications. It is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio supports memory-intensive tasks, though storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached storage due to the absence of local NVMe drives.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six vCPUs, high-speed network bandwidth, and substantial memory capacity for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing a 1:8 resource ratio.
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking.
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded workloads.
